title: Distressed Seller - Emotional Situation
clientGreeting: Hi... I'm going through a divorce and I need to sell our house quickly. It's been a difficult time and honestly, I'm stressed about the whole thing. I'm not sure what to do or what it's worth.
openingContext: A distressed client is dealing with a divorce and needs to sell quickly. They are emotional and need both practical guidance and emotional support.
clientResponses: Yeah... we've been living in the house for 12 years and raising our kids here. It's hard to think about leaving it. But we both need to move forward with our lives.
I don't really know much about real estate. My ex knows more about these things, but they're not willing to help. I just need someone I can trust to guide me through this.
That would help a lot. I need someone who understands what I'm going through. When could we meet? I want to make sure I'm getting a fair deal.

clientProfile.name: Jennifer Davis
clientProfile.budget: Home valued at $350,000 - needs fair market value
clientProfile.discDescription: Steady, supportive, emotionally vulnerable, needs reassurance
keyTraits: Slow to open up but loyal
painPoints: Fear of being taken advantage of
motivations: Make clean break and move forward

evaluationCriteria.emotionalIntelligence: Did ISA show empathy and understanding for client's situation?
evaluationCriteria.trustBuilding: Did ISA establish trust and confidence?
evaluationCriteria.professionalCompassion: Did ISA balance support with professional expertise?
evaluationCriteria.guidanceProvision: Did ISA provide clear guidance for decisions?
evaluationCriteria.supportiveApproach: Did ISA minimize stress and provide reassurance?
evaluationCriteria.appointmentClosing: Did ISA secure a home evaluation or meeting?
